content: A message was moved here from #containers > help in dev by Philip Durbin 🚀.
@QOLAC hi! Could you please try the quickstart at https://guides.dataverse.org/en/6.10.1/container/running/demo.html#quickstart and let me know if that works for you?
@QOLAC also, we have a meeting next week if you're like to join. Please see #containers > meetings

@Philip Durbin 🚀 Hi!
The demo is working perfectly on my end — thank you for that. I wanted to ask if there is a production-ready compose.yml file available that includes proxy and SSL support. I would appreciate it if you could share one.

I have added the Zoom meeting scheduled for April 15, titled “Webinar: Configure Your Dataverse Repository to Register DataCite DOIs”, to my calendar.

I also attempted to submit an abstract for the Dataverse Community Meeting 2026 via the submission portal. However, even after entering my contact details, I am still seeing the message: “To submit your abstract, first add your ‘Contact details’ in the menu bar.” Please let me know if there is anything I might be missing.

If you were referring to a different meeting, I would be happy to join — please feel free to share the details.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

@QOLAC Hi! I'm talking about this meeting...

2026-04-16, 09:30 Eastern meeting notes

... of the Containerization Working Group: https://ct.gdcc.io

Again, you're welcome to join! We love hearing feedback from people using our containers. I already put this topic on the agenda. :smile:

QOLAC said:

I have added the Zoom meeting scheduled for April 15, titled “Webinar: Configure Your Dataverse Repository to Register DataCite DOIs”, to my calendar.

The above should be interesting, but it probably won't help you with containers. :sweat_smile:

QOLAC said:

I also attempted to submit an abstract for the Dataverse Community Meeting 2026

For the above I would suggest emailing dcm2026@bcocongresos.com

There's also a topic at #community > #Dataverse2026 you might like but for help, emailing is probably better.

QOLAC said:

I wanted to ask if there is a production-ready compose.yml file available that includes proxy and SSL support.

No. We definitely need this! You can find some discussion here: Consider Traefik or similar for HTTPS etc. (milestone E) #10359

We also have a mostly empty topic here: #containers > add Traefik

And a related topic: #containers > guidance container for production
